name: L-Carnitine
subtitle: Amino Acid Derivative | Fatty Acid Transporter
overview: L-Carnitine is an amino acid derivative naturally produced in the body and essential for transporting long-chain fatty acids into mitochondria for energy production. Injectable forms bypass digestive absorption limitations of oral supplements.
mechanism: Acts as a carrier molecule, shuttling long-chain fatty acids across the inner mitochondrial membrane via the carnitine palmitoyltransferase (CPT) system. This is the rate-limiting step in fatty acid oxidation for energy production.
keyBenefits: Enhanced fatty acid transport to mitochondria

items.name: Fatty Acid Oxidation
items.description: Primary function transporting fatty acids into mitochondria for beta-oxidation.

items.name: Energy Production
items.description: Supports ATP generation from fat metabolism, particularly during exercise.

items.name: Weight Management
items.description: May enhance fat utilization when combined with exercise and caloric deficit.

items.name: Exercise Recovery
items.description: May reduce muscle damage markers and improve recovery post-exercise.

items.name: Endurance Support
items.description: Enhances fat utilization during prolonged exercise, potentially sparing glycogen.

items.name: Carnitine Deficiency
items.description: FDA-approved treatment for primary and secondary carnitine deficiency.

items.name: Dialysis Support
items.description: Used in patients on hemodialysis who develop carnitine depletion.

deliveryMethods.overview: Subcutaneous or intramuscular injection provides superior bioavailability compared to oral supplementation, which is limited to ~15-20% absorption.

steps: Allow vial to reach room temperature
Clean rubber stopper with alcohol swab
Gently swirl to dissolve; never shake
Label with date and store refrigerated at 2-8°C

interactions.notes: L-Carnitine may affect thyroid hormone action. Those on thyroid medication should monitor levels.
May enhance effects of warfarin and similar medications.

timeline.effects: Injectable form provides rapid absorption within 30-60 minutes
Potential improvements in energy and exercise capacity
Cumulative benefits on fat metabolism and recovery
Full metabolic optimization when combined with exercise

common: Body odor (fishy smell at high doses)
monitoring: Generally well-tolerated with established safety profile
Monitor thyroid function if on thyroid medication

good: Certificate of Analysis with purity testing

references.title: L-Carnitine Supplementation and Exercise
references.keyFindings: Meta-analysis showing modest benefits of L-carnitine on exercise performance and recovery markers.

references.title: Carnitine Metabolism and Function
references.keyFindings: Comprehensive review of carnitine's role in fatty acid transport and energy metabolism.
